text:
Centre for Neurology Services

Neurosciences Division

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025

Dr Hannah Clarke
Riverside Health Centre
12 Market Lane, Bristol BS1 4QH

Dear Dr Hannah Clarke,

James Turner, DOB: 23-11-1989, Hospital No: H3928471 NHS No. 8471295630
Flat 3, 21 Dock Street, Bristol, BS1 6AB

Work and Fit Note Guidance

Background: Patient works as a warehouse picker between tall racking and narrow aisles; tasks include variable lifting and pace depending on shift.

Current plan:
- Occupational adjustments advised: avoid climbing tasks, ensure clear aisle access, and schedule rest breaks during high‑pace shifts.
- Fit note issuing: If functional limitations prevent safe performance of stock picking, lifting above shoulder height, or rapid task pacing, please issue a Med 3 stating “May be fit for work” with adjustments (no ladder work, no working at height, team-lift for items >15kg, reduced exposure to fast‑moving equipment, and scheduled breaks).
- Duration: Initial fit note for up to 4 weeks, with review thereafter. If adjustments cannot be accommodated, tick “Not fit for work” for the same period.
- Employer liaison: Patient to provide the note to line manager and HR. If needed, refer to Occupational Health for a workplace assessment.
- Certification: A GP or hospital clinician can issue the Med 3. Backdating is permissible to the date the condition affected work capacity if clinically justified. Document functional impact and safety considerations in records.
- Review: Telephone review in 4 weeks to reassess work tolerance and need for ongoing adjustments.

Please contact the clinic if further clarification is required.

Yours sincerely,

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: I reviewed the letter content thoroughly. It focuses on occupational adjustments, fit note guidance, and work capacity in a warehouse setting. There is no mention of epilepsy, seizures, or any seizure frequency. As seizures are not referenced at all, the correct label is 'no seizure frequency reference'.
reference: Create a reasonable NHS letter with administrative guidance on the fit note issuing process related to epilepsy

flaws.description: No seizure frequency referenced in gold
Row marked as not OK in raw dataset
